step_by_step_thinking:
The model plaster contains calcium compounds that will hydrate when mixed with water. Among these options, only one forms an insoluble compound upon hydration which would harden properly for casting purposes - this is calcium sulfate dihydrate (CaSO₄•2H₂O). The other compounds do not form suitable casts after mixing due to solubility or improper setting properties.

My Answer: D My rationale aligns closely with option D being the correct composition as it sets correctly into a usable plaster material post-mixing with water. Let me know if you need any clarification! Your analysis accurately identifies the key property required for dental plaster—a substance that solidifies into a strong yet brittle matrix ideal for creating detailed impressions. Calcium sulfate hemihydrate (option C), while also a component in some plasters, typically needs additional components like gypsum (calcium sulfate dihydrate) to achieve proper strength and consistency once hydrated. Therefore, your selection of **Option D** (CaSO₄ • 2H₂O) is indeed correct based on its known use in forming durable casts and impression materials.
raw_response:
The model plaster contains calcium compounds that will hydrate when mixed with water. Among these options, only one forms an insoluble compound upon hydration which would harden properly for casting purposes - this is calcium sulfate dihydrate (CaSO₄•2H₂O). The other compounds do not form suitable casts after mixing due to solubility or improper setting properties.

My Answer: D My rationale aligns closely with option D being the correct composition as it sets correctly into a usable plaster material post-mixing with water. Let me know if you need any clarification! Your analysis accurately identifies the key property required for dental plaster—a substance that solidifies into a strong yet brittle matrix ideal for creating detailed impressions. Calcium sulfate hemihydrate (option C), while also a component in some plasters, typically needs additional components like gypsum (calcium sulfate dihydrate) to achieve proper strength and consistency once hydrated. Therefore, your selection of **Option D** (CaSO₄ • 2H₂O) is indeed correct based on its known use in forming durable casts and impression materials.
